author: @fetchitnowuk
text: Ok. Well I tried and sort of failed. I used Steve's suggestion about the electrical tape and painted the ink. I cooked the wine bottle for about 20 seconds. Now the bottle cracked majority along the ink but not all the way round. Therefore should I cook for longer.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah defo - we tried half a dozen bottle and jars - two were rubbish the rest were super cool!

author: @fetchitnowuk
text: Going to try this shortly. Lets hope I dont make a mess of the kitchen.

author: @samfosteriam
text: I would love for the conductive ink / microwave to be quick and effective way to get holes into glass - by just painting on a dot or ring. But I'm guessing the uneven heat and tensions will cause unpredictable shattering. I'll give it a go anyhow though.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Ro Ho
for cutting tiny holes - not very well

author: @trailkeeper
text: Can use various grinding and sharpening stones to smooth the edges.  It seems glass tends to actually cut into some sharpening stones, so an alternative is to find cheap stones and rocks with different textures such as sandstone.  Hard stones will have little effect since they are too smooth like glass.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thanks for that mate - very useful
